key_themes: Resilient FY2025 profitability and RoTE outperformance
Generous dividends, buybacks, and capital returns post-privatization
Solid 2026 guidance despite net interest income pressure from rates
sources_checked: Yahoo Finance, Investing.com, MarketBeat, WSJ/Morningstar analyst summaries
AIB Investor Relations results and guidance
summary: AIB Group delivered €2.1 billion in FY2025 profit after tax with strong capital returns (total distributions €2.25bn) and shares reacted positively to resilient margins and >20% RoTE guidance for 2026, alongside expected 5% loan growth. Analyst consensus is Buy (5 Buy / 8 Hold, average PT ~€10.0–10.3 vs current ~€9.8), supported by high dividend yield (~8–9%), share buybacks, SRT capital benefits, and completion of privatization. The biggest risk is sustained low/declining interest rates causing further NII compression, plus competition from digital banks and modest forecasted earnings decline.[[1]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/LON/AIBG/)[[2]](https://www.investing.com/news/company-news/aib-group-fy-2025-slides-25-returns-despite-rate-headwinds-93CH-4540354)[[3]](https://www.investing.com/equities/allied-irish-b-consensus-estimates)

recent_posts.highlight: AIB $A5G continues to exceed its 15% ROTE target. Can the bank maintain this outperformance?
J.P. Morgan upgrades AIB Group to overweight, PT €10.70, citing Ireland macro tailwinds and undervalued yields.
Lists $AIBG among best Irish stocks to buy for Saint Patrick’s Day portfolio.
Shares rise on stabilized NII in Q3/FY results; strong profitability, final dividend proposed at €0.46257/share, ROT E >20% guidance for 2026.
